title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 67.
content:
There was an Old Person of Tring,
    Who embellished his nose with a ring;
    He gazed at the moon,
    Every evening in June,
    That ecstatic Old Person of Tring.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 67.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
